task_completion.description: Create a title page with the collection title, dates, and archive name.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the text 'The Sterling Radiator Company Records', '1922-1965', and 'Industrial History Archive of Cleveland' on the first page of the document in OpenOffice Writer.

task_completion.description: Insert an automated Table of Contents.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a Table of Contents block in the document, typically displaying headings with page numbers and often having a gray background indicating it is an automated field.

task_completion.description: Add page numbers to the document.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for page numbers visible in the header or footer area of the document pages.

task_completion.description: Organize the document into the required main sections.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the specific headings: 'Descriptive Summary', 'Biographical/Historical Note', 'Scope and Contents', and 'Container List' in the document.

task_completion.description: Organize items by Series and present them in tables within the Container List.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for tables under the 'Container List' section, with columns for 'Box', 'Folder', 'Title', and 'Date', grouped under Series headings.

task_completion.description: Save the completed document as Sterling_Finding_Aid.odt.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the title bar of the OpenOffice Writer window to see 'Sterling_Finding_Aid.odt' or check a file manager showing the saved file in the Documents folder.

integrity.description: The agent used Apache OpenOffice Writer to create the document.
integrity.visual_evidence: Visual evidence of the OpenOffice Writer interface (toolbars, menus) being actively used to edit the document.

integrity.description: The document was not generated using terminal commands or scripts.
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal windows showing commands like python scripts or pandoc being used to create the .odt file directly.

integrity.description: The tables in the document are genuine table objects.
integrity.visual_evidence: The tables have visible gridlines or table formatting characteristic of OpenOffice Writer tables, rather than plain text aligned with spaces or tabs.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The document must contain exactly 4 main sections (Descriptive Summary, Biographical/Historical Note, Scope and Contents, Container List). The Container List must have 4 series subsections (Series I: Administrative Records, Series II: Financial Records, Series III: Marketing and Sales, Series IV: Technical Drawings). There should be exactly 4 tables corresponding to these series, with a total of 15 inventory items (4, 4, 4, and 3 items respectively). The title page must include 'The Sterling Radiator Company Records', '1922-1965', and 'Industrial History Archive of Cleveland'. The file must be saved as Sterling_Finding_Aid.odt.
